positive

# Expected behavior under T1 + T2:
#
# Title-only task with five coordination-heavy constraints: exactly
# 12 words AND total length between 60 and 80 characters AND must
# contain the phrase "workflow orchestration" AND must end with a
# question mark AND must not contain a colon or a semicolon.
#
# The "workflow orchestration" requirement consumes 22 of the 80-char
# budget, leaving ~58 characters for 10 other words plus spaces and
# the question mark. The no-colon-or-semicolon rule prevents the
# proposer from wedging in a sub-title structure to manage word count
# independently of the rest. The 12-word and 60-80-char constraints
# coupled with the fixed phrase requirement create real tension:
# satisfying word count tends to push character count over the cap,
# satisfying character count tends to drop the word count below 12
# or push the fixed phrase out of position.
#
# Cycle 1: proposer drafts a title that misses one or more
# constraints. Reviewer cites the specific length/count violations
# (e.g., "title is 13 words and 84 characters; word count must be
# exactly 12 and character count must be 60-80").
#
# Cycle 2: proposer redrafts to address feedback. Hitting all
# constraints simultaneously is hard; the redraft likely fixes one
# while breaking another. Reviewer cycle 2 cites the persisting
# defect class (length/count/phrase compliance). Same material issue
# persists, satisfying the F2 stuck wording verbatim.
#
# Cycle 3 (if reached): same pattern. Judge eventually fires `stuck`
# because the title cannot stabilize across all constraints
# simultaneously.
#
# Expected trajectory: [iterate, stuck] or [iterate, iterate, stuck].
# Classifier label: match.
#
# Persistence pattern: repeated length/count/phrase violations on
# the same constraint set. Same defect class, possibly different
# specific values per cycle, but same material failure mode.
#
# Stuck-correct condition: stuck cited "length", "word count",
# "character count", "workflow orchestration phrase position",
# or similar verbatim defect descriptions across cycles. Confirms
# the stuck call was based on real artifact content, not on
# transitively adopted prior claims.
#
# Failure modes to watch for:
# - If [accept] on cycle 1: task is too easy. Tighten the character
#   window (e.g., 60-72 inclusive) or add another structural
#   constraint.
# - If stuck fires correctly: confirm reviewer/judge cited the
#   defects from the current title verbatim across cycles. The
#   reviewer should NOT restate cycle-1 numbers while reading
#   cycle-2's title; T1 instructs the reviewer to recount.
# - If T1+T2 over-corrected and stuck never fires despite genuine
#   coupled failure: examine prompts and outputs to confirm whether
#   the constraint set was actually verified or hand-waved. Reviewer
#   or judge ignoring real defects in the artifact would indicate
#   T1+T2 instructions are too aggressive against legitimate
#   persistence claims.
